description: 30/40 (75%) — first run from eguard-agent/ with separate autoresearch.sh. No conflict with macOS agent. Server builds from fe_eguard/go, battery runs from fe_eguard/scripts/. All guardrails green: agent 29.7MB, server 177MB, checks pass.

asi.hypothesis: Separate autoresearch.sh in eguard-agent/ avoids file conflict with macOS agent in fe_eguard/
asi.result: 30/40 (75%). Works cleanly. No more concurrency conflicts.
asi.architecture: Windows: eguard-agent/autoresearch.sh builds from fe_eguard/go, runs fe_eguard/scripts/windows-attack-battery.sh. macOS: fe_eguard/autoresearch.sh runs fe_eguard/scripts/macos-attack-battery.sh. Both share the same server binary and IOC rules.

description: 32/40 (80%). 24 distinct rules, 344 high/critical. Server 191MB. All guardrails green. Sawtooth climbing: 75→80.

asi.hypothesis: Stable cycle characterization from eguard-agent harness

description: 29/40 (72.5%). Server restarted (191→112MB). 25 distinct rules. All guardrails green. Sawtooth: 75→80→72.5.

description: 32/40 (80%) but checks failed: macOS agent added serverSideIOCRuleFromThreatIntelRuleWithReason between our build and checks. Now resolved — function exists in working tree.

asi.rollback_reason: Transient checks failure from macOS agent's concurrent edits to server_side_ioc.go. The function now exists.
